text:
HEMATOLOGY / BLOOD AND MARROW TRANSPLANT PROGRAM
OUTPATIENT TRANSPLANT CLINIC NOTE
DAY +30 POST-ALLOGENEIC HEMATOPOIETIC STEM CELL TRANSPLANT ASSESSMENT

---

CHIEF COMPLAINT:
Ms. Renata Kowalczyk is a 48-year-old female with FLT3-ITD (high allelic ratio) AML, FAB M4, ELN Adverse risk, presenting for her scheduled day +30 post-transplant clinic visit to assess engraftment, donor chimerism, infectious complications, and early post-transplant toxicities.

---

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Ms. Kowalczyk is a 48-year-old non-smoking female with de novo acute myeloid leukemia (AML, FAB M4 / acute myelomonocytic), initially presenting approximately 10 weeks ago with a 3-week history of profound fatigue, dyspnea on exertion, spontaneous gingival bleeding, easy bruising, drenching night sweats, and a white blood cell count of 87,400/microliter with 74% blasts on bone marrow biopsy. Molecular profiling at diagnosis identified FLT3-ITD with a high allelic ratio (VAF 82%), co-occurring with DNMT3A R882H; NPM1, IDH1/IDH2, and CEBPA were wild-type. Cytogenetics showed a normal female karyotype (46,XX). She was classified as ELN 2022 Adverse risk and enrolled on the 7+3 plus midostaurin induction protocol (cytarabine 200 mg/m2/day continuous infusion days 1-7; daunorubicin 60 mg/m2 on days 1, 2, 3; midostaurin 50 mg twice daily days 8-21). Her induction course was complicated by febrile neutropenia with E. coli bacteremia (managed with IV ceftriaxone), grade 3 oral mucositis, and a 22-day neutropenia period. Day +28 bone marrow biopsy confirmed complete morphologic remission (CR) with MRD negativity by 8-color multiparameter flow cytometry (sensitivity 0.01%) and undetectable FLT3-ITD by PCR. Given her high-risk molecular profile, she was counseled extensively regarding allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation (alloHSCT) in first CR as the definitive consolidative strategy.

HLA typing revealed no matched sibling donors (both siblings were mismatched). An NMDP/Be The Match unrelated donor search rapidly identified a 10/10 allele-level matched unrelated donor (MUD), a 38-year-old CMV-negative male. Following bridging consolidation with one cycle of intermediate-dose cytarabine (IDAC; cytarabine 1.5 g/m2 IV q12h on days 1, 3, and 5), complicated by expected grade 2 bilateral conjunctivitis and grade 1 peripheral neuropathy (both resolved), transplant evaluation was completed and she was admitted approximately 14 weeks from diagnosis for myeloablative allogeneic HSCT.

Conditioning consisted of fludarabine 40 mg/m2/day IV on days -6 through -3 combined with busulfan 3.2 mg/kg/day IV on days -6 through -3 (FluBu4 myeloablative regimen), with pharmacokinetic dose adjustment to target AUC 4500-6000 micromol*min/L. GVHD prophylaxis included tacrolimus (started day -3, target trough 8-12 ng/mL), short-course MTX (15 mg/m2 day +1; 10 mg/m2 days +3, +6, +11), and rabbit anti-thymocyte globulin (ATG/Thymoglobulin 2.5 mg/kg/day on days -3, -2, -1; total 7.5 mg/kg). Peripheral blood stem cells were infused on day 0 without adverse reactions; CD34+ cell dose was 6.8 x 10^6/kg.

The post-transplant period (days +7 through +18) was marked by expected but significant early toxicities: grade 3 oral/GI mucositis (managed with IV morphine PCA and total parenteral nutrition for 8 days), grade 2 nausea and vomiting, febrile neutropenia on day +8 (blood cultures negative; treated empirically with IV meropenem with clinical resolution), and mild sinusoidal obstruction syndrome (peak bilirubin 2.8 mg/dL and ALT 142 U/L on day +12, meeting Modified Seattle Criteria for mild SOS; managed conservatively with fluid restriction and weight monitoring, resolving by day +20 without defibrotide). Weekly CMV surveillance by quantitative PCR was initiated at day +14 given the CMV+/- donor-recipient pairing. CMV viremia was first detected at day +28 (460 IU/mL), and pre-emptive therapy with valganciclovir was started. The patient was discharged from the inpatient unit and presents today for her first major outpatient follow-up visit.

She reports markedly improved energy compared to the peak of her mucositis. She is eating and drinking adequately by mouth, having fully resumed an oral diet approximately 10 days ago. She reports mild fatigue with moderate exertion, which she acknowledges is an improvement from her inpatient experience. No fever, chills, or rigors since day +22. No diarrhea, no significant abdominal pain. She notes mild residual oral sensitivity but no frank mucositis or ulcers. She is taking valganciclovir, tacrolimus, trimethoprim-sulfamethoxazole, acyclovir, and fluconazole by mouth without difficulty. She reports no rash, no skin changes, no yellowing of eyes or skin, and no leg swelling. Appetite is approximately 75% of baseline. She denies any new bleeding, bruising, headache, or visual changes.

---

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
Constitutional: Mild fatigue with moderate exertion; no fever, night sweats, or chills since day +22. Weight has been stable per home scale over the past week.
HEENT: No oral pain; residual mild oral dryness. No epistaxis. No visual changes. No headache.
Cardiorespiratory: No dyspnea at rest; mild dyspnea on exertion (climbing one flight of stairs); no cough, no chest pain, no palpitations.
Gastrointestinal: Appetite recovering; tolerating oral diet; no nausea today; no vomiting; no diarrhea; no hematochezia or melena; no abdominal pain.
Genitourinary: No hematuria; no dysuria; no urinary frequency changes.
Musculoskeletal: Mild generalized deconditioning; no joint pain or swelling.
Skin: No new rashes; no skin changes; no pruritus.
Neurologic: No focal neurologic symptoms; no paresthesias (prior grade 1 hand tingling from IDAC resolved).
Psychiatric: Patient reports good mood and high motivation; mild anxiety about disease status.
Endocrine: No symptoms to suggest adrenal insufficiency.

---

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY:
1. Acute myeloid leukemia, FAB M4 (acute myelomonocytic), de novo, FLT3-ITD high allelic ratio (VAF 82%), DNMT3A R882H co-mutation, normal karyotype (46,XX), ELN Adverse risk - diagnosed approximately 14 weeks ago
2. Type 2 diabetes mellitus - managed with metformin prior to transplant (held during transplant admission; insulin used as needed during inpatient stay)
3. Mild hypertension - baseline, well-controlled
4. Premature ovarian insufficiency (expected from conditioning regimen)

PRIOR ONCOLOGIC TREATMENTS:
1. Induction chemotherapy: Cytarabine 200 mg/m2/day x7 days + daunorubicin 60 mg/m2/day x3 days + midostaurin 50 mg BID days 8-21 (7+3 + midostaurin)
2. Bridging consolidation: IDAC (cytarabine 1.5 g/m2 IV q12h days 1, 3, 5) + midostaurin days 8-21
3. Myeloablative alloHSCT: FluBu4 conditioning (fludarabine/busulfan); 10/10 MUD peripheral blood stem cells; GVHD prophylaxis tacrolimus/MTX/ATG

SOCIAL HISTORY:
High school biology teacher; married; two adult children. Non-smoker, no alcohol use. Currently on medical leave from work. Lives with husband. Supportive home environment; husband present at clinic today.

FAMILY HISTORY:
No family history of leukemia, MDS, or other hematologic malignancies. No family history of breast, colon, or other solid organ cancers known.

ALLERGIES:
NKDA (no known drug allergies). No known contrast allergies.

MEDICATIONS (Current):
1. Tacrolimus (oral capsules) - immunosuppression for GVHD prophylaxis; dose adjusted to trough target 8-12 ng/mL
2. Valganciclovir 900 mg orally twice daily - pre-emptive therapy for CMV viremia (started day +28)
3. Trimethoprim-sulfamethoxazole (TMP-SMX) DS 1 tablet orally three times per week - Pneumocystis jirovecii pneumonia (PCP) prophylaxis
4. Acyclovir 400 mg orally twice daily - HSV/VZV prophylaxis
5. Fluconazole 100 mg orally daily - antifungal prophylaxis
6. Ondansetron 4 mg orally as needed - nausea (PRN; using rarely)
7. Magnesium oxide 400 mg orally twice daily - replacement for tacrolimus-related hypomagnesemia
8. Potassium chloride 20 mEq orally twice daily - electrolyte replacement
9. Omeprazole 20 mg orally daily - GI mucosal protection
10. Metformin 500 mg orally twice daily - Type 2 diabetes (restarted with caution given renal function monitoring)
11. Calcium carbonate 500 mg + Vitamin D 400 IU orally twice daily - bone health during immunosuppression

---

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
General: Alert, oriented, appears well and in no acute distress. Mild pallor. Better appearing than during inpatient admission.
Vital Signs: Temperature 36.8°C, Blood Pressure 124/76 mmHg, Heart Rate 82 bpm (regular), Respiratory Rate 16 breaths/min, SpO2 97% on room air. Weight 62.5 kg (baseline pre-illness 67 kg; 6.7% below baseline, but improved from lowest inpatient weight of ~58 kg).
ECOG Performance Status: 1 (restricted in physically strenuous activity but ambulatory and able to carry out light work).
HEENT: Normocephalic, atraumatic. Anicteric sclerae. Conjunctivae with mild pallor, no injection or discharge. Oropharynx: mild residual erythema of the posterior oropharynx but no frank mucositis, ulceration, or white plaques. Lips intact.
Neck: Supple, no lymphadenopathy. No JVD.
Cardiovascular: Regular rate and rhythm. Normal S1 and S2. No murmurs, rubs, or gallops. No peripheral edema.
Respiratory: Lungs clear to auscultation bilaterally. No wheezing, crackles, or rhonchi.
Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. Normoactive bowel sounds. Liver not enlarged (span ~10 cm by percussion). Spleen not palpable. No ascites.
Skin: No rash, no petechiae, no ecchymoses. No lichenoid changes. No jaundice. PICC site (right arm): clean, no erythema, no induration, no discharge.
Extremities: No edema. No cyanosis. Peripheral pulses intact bilaterally.
Neurologic: Cranial nerves II-XII intact. No cerebellar signs. Gait steady. No peripheral sensory deficits on brief screening exam.
Lymph nodes: No peripheral lymphadenopathy in cervical, axillary, or inguinal chains.

---

LABORATORY RESULTS:
Complete Blood Count (Day +30):
  WBC: 4,200/microliter
  ANC: 3,100/microliter
  Hemoglobin: 10.2 g/dL
  MCV: 94 fL
  Platelets: 112,000/microliter
  No blasts identified on differential
  Differential: Neutrophils 74%, Lymphocytes 17%, Monocytes 8%, Eosinophils 1%, Basophils 0%

Comprehensive Metabolic Panel:
  Sodium: 138 mEq/L
  Potassium: 3.6 mEq/L (on replacement)
  Chloride: 104 mEq/L
  CO2: 25 mEq/L
  BUN: 18 mg/dL
  Creatinine: 0.94 mg/dL (eGFR ~74 mL/min/1.73m2; improved from peak creatinine 1.18 inpatient)
  Glucose: 118 mg/dL (fasting)
  Calcium: 9.0 mg/dL
  Magnesium: 1.7 mEq/L (low-normal, on replacement)
  Phosphorus: 3.2 mg/dL
  Total Bilirubin: 0.9 mg/dL (normalized from peak 2.8 mg/dL)
  Direct Bilirubin: 0.2 mg/dL
  ALT: 38 U/L (normalized from peak 142 U/L; prior mild SOS resolved)
  AST: 28 U/L
  Alkaline Phosphatase: 94 U/L
  Total Protein: 6.4 g/dL
  Albumin: 3.6 g/dL
  Uric Acid: 4.8 mg/dL

Tacrolimus Trough Level (drawn this morning, 12 hours post-dose):
  Tacrolimus: 9.2 ng/mL (within target range 8-12 ng/mL)

CMV Surveillance (quantitative PCR, peripheral blood):
  Day +28 result (available today): 460 IU/mL (detected; pre-emptive valganciclovir started)
  Day +35 result (obtained at today's visit): 210 IU/mL (declining on valganciclovir - favorable trend)

EBV PCR (peripheral blood): Undetectable
HHV-6 PCR: Undetectable
BK virus urine PCR: Not performed (no hematuria)

Chimerism Analysis (peripheral blood, day +28 STR analysis, resulted today):
  Overall donor chimerism: 99% donor / <1% residual host cells
  CD3+ T-cell fraction: 97% donor
  CD33+ myeloid fraction: 100% donor
  Interpretation: Essentially complete donor engraftment

Engraftment Milestones (retrospective from inpatient documentation):
  Neutrophil engraftment (ANC >500/microliter x3 consecutive days): Day +14
  Platelet engraftment (platelets >50,000/microliter unsupported): Day +22

Urinalysis: No protein, no glucose, no hematuria, no casts

HbA1c: 7.4% (mildly elevated from baseline 7.2% at diagnosis; consistent with tacrolimus-related insulin resistance; metformin restarted cautiously, monitoring renal function)

---

IMAGING RESULTS:
No new imaging obtained at today's visit. Most recent prior imaging:
- Pre-transplant PET-CT (approximately 7 weeks prior to transplant admission): No FDG-avid foci suggestive of extramedullary leukemic disease. Splenomegaly had resolved (spleen 11.8 cm). Cleared for transplant.
- CT chest/abdomen/pelvis at AML diagnosis (approximately 14 weeks ago): Mild splenomegaly (14.2 cm) at that time, since resolved. No extramedullary disease.
Next surveillance CT chest/abdomen/pelvis tentatively planned for approximately day +90 post-transplant.

---

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:

Ms. Kowalczyk is a 48-year-old woman with de novo AML, FAB M4, FLT3-ITD high allelic ratio (ELN Adverse risk), who achieved complete morphologic and MRD-negative remission after 7+3 plus midostaurin induction and subsequently underwent myeloablative alloHSCT from a 10/10 MUD with FluBu4 conditioning and tacrolimus/Trexall/ATG GVHD prophylaxis. Today's day +30 assessment is overall very encouraging.

1. ENGRAFTMENT - FULL DONOR CHIMERISM ACHIEVED:
Excellent engraftment demonstrated. Neutrophil engraftment occurred on day +14 and platelet engraftment on day +22, both within expected windows for a PBSC graft with this conditioning platform. Today's CBC shows a WBC of 4,200 with an ANC of 3,100 and platelets of 112,000, reflecting robust early count recovery. Peripheral blood chimerism at day +28 confirms 99% donor engraftment by STR analysis, with 100% donor in the myeloid fraction - this is essentially complete donor engraftment and is highly reassuring. No further chimerism testing is required at this early timepoint beyond confirming today's result.
 - Plan: Repeat peripheral blood chimerism at approximately day +90 and day +150. Bone marrow biopsy with MRD assessment and cytogenetics planned at approximately day +60 per protocol.

2. DISEASE STATUS - COMPLETE REMISSION, MAINTAINED:
The patient remains in complete remission by CBC morphology (no circulating blasts) and clinical assessment. She has no constitutional symptoms to suggest relapse (no fever, night sweats, new bone pain, or unexplained cytopenias). No lymphadenopathy or organomegaly on exam. Given the high-risk molecular profile (FLT3-ITD, ELN Adverse) and the well-recognized early relapse risk in this subgroup, vigilance is paramount. The outstanding MRD status at the time of transplant and the rapidly established full donor chimerism are both favorable prognostic indicators at this early post-transplant juncture.
 - Plan: Bone marrow biopsy with multiparameter flow cytometry MRD (sensitivity 0.01%), FLT3-ITD PCR, DNMT3A R882H ddPCR, and cytogenetics (to confirm XY donor karyotype) at day +60. Any new unexplained cytopenias or falling chimerism will prompt urgent marrow evaluation.

3. CMV VIREMIA - PRE-EMPTIVE TREATMENT INITIATED, RESPONDING:
CMV viremia was detected at day +28 (460 IU/mL) in the setting of this CMV+/- (recipient positive/donor negative) pairing, which carries a well-established higher risk of CMV reactivation. Pre-emptive valganciclovir 900 mg twice daily was appropriately started. Today's repeat CMV PCR drawn at day +35 returns at 210 IU/mL - a reassuring decline by more than 50% over one week, suggesting good virologic response to valganciclovir. The patient reports no CMV end-organ symptoms (no visual changes, no diarrhea, no dyspnea).
 - Plan: Continue valganciclovir 900 mg PO BID. Recheck CMV PCR weekly. Target two consecutive undetectable results before considering discontinuation. Monitor CBC weekly given valganciclovir myelosuppressive potential; adjust dose if cytopenias develop. Counsel patient to report any visual symptoms, increased diarrhea, or respiratory changes.
 - Note: Tacrolimus-related immunosuppression and the CMV-naive donor graft are the primary drivers of reactivation risk in this patient. The risk should diminish as tacrolimus is tapered and as donor-derived CMV-specific T-cell immunity reconstitutes over the coming months.

4. ACUTE GVHD - NONE IDENTIFIED:
The patient has no current signs or symptoms of acute GVHD. Skin: no rash. Liver: bilirubin and transaminases have normalized (prior mild bilirubin elevation on day +12 was attributed to mild SOS, now fully resolved). GI: no diarrhea, no nausea requiring intervention. The ATG incorporated into her conditioning, combined with tacrolimus/Trexall prophylaxis, appears to have been effective. This is a favorable finding at day +30. Risk for acute GVHD extends through day +100, and the patient and her husband have been counseled to report any new rash, jaundice, diarrhea, or abdominal cramping promptly.
 - Plan: Tacrolimus continued at current dose. Trough target remains 8-12 ng/mL through at least day +90, then reassess for taper initiation based on GVHD status and chimerism. Educate patient on signs of acute and chronic GVHD.

5. SINUSOIDAL OBSTRUCTION SYNDROME (SOS/VOD) - RESOLVED:
Mild SOS (bilirubin peak 2.8 mg/dL, ALT 142 U/L on day +12, meeting Modified Seattle Criteria for mild disease) managed conservatively with fluid restriction and weight monitoring during inpatient stay. Bilirubin today is 0.9 mg/dL and ALT 38 U/L - complete biochemical resolution. No defibrotide was required. No further SOS monitoring needed at this time.

6. ELECTROLYTES AND RENAL FUNCTION - TACROLIMUS-RELATED ISSUES:
Known tacrolimus-related hypomagnesemia (magnesium 1.7 mEq/L, low-normal) and mild hypokalemia (potassium 3.6 mEq/L) are being managed with ongoing oral replacement. Creatinine 0.94 mg/dL with eGFR ~74 mL/min/1.73m2 represents improvement from inpatient peak creatinine (1.18 mg/dL), likely reflecting recovery from ATG administration and early tacrolimus effects. Renal function will remain under close surveillance; if eGFR deteriorates below 60 mL/min, tacrolimus dose adjustment and nephrology consultation will be considered.
 - Plan: Continue electrolyte supplementation. Monitor BMP, tacrolimus trough, and creatinine weekly.

7. TYPE 2 DIABETES / GLYCEMIC MANAGEMENT:
Metformin 500 mg BID carefully restarted after confirming adequate renal function (eGFR >60). HbA1c today at 7.4% is mildly elevated from her baseline of 7.2%, likely reflecting tacrolimus-induced insulin resistance and the metabolic stress of transplant. Fasting glucose of 118 mg/dL is acceptable. No symptomatic hyperglycemia.
 - Plan: Continue metformin 500 mg BID; up-titration can be considered at next visit if tolerated and renal function remains stable. If persistent hyperglycemia on tacrolimus, may need to involve endocrinology or add a second agent. Home blood glucose monitoring encouraged.

8. NUTRITIONAL RECOVERY AND WEIGHT:
Weight today is 62.5 kg, down approximately 6.7% from her pre-illness weight of 67 kg but significantly improved from her nadir weight during inpatient admission. She has fully resumed oral diet. Appetite at 75% of baseline is acceptable at day +30. Oral mucositis has resolved. She is no longer on TPN.
 - Plan: Encourage high-protein, high-calorie diet. Dietary counseling referral provided. If weight does not recover over next 4-6 weeks, reassess with nutrition services.

9. INFECTIOUS PROPHYLAXIS:
Current prophylaxis regimen: TMP-SMX (PCP prophylaxis), acyclovir (HSV/VZV prophylaxis), fluconazole (antifungal prophylaxis). This will be maintained while patient is on tacrolimus immunosuppression. Valganciclovir is serving dual purpose of CMV pre-emptive treatment and additional herpesvirus coverage. No antifungal escalation needed at this time (no radiographic changes suggestive of mold infection, no fever).

10. PERFORMANCE STATUS AND FUNCTIONAL RECOVERY:
ECOG PS 1 today - patient is ambulatory, managing her ADLs independently at home, and reports improved energy trajectory. She is not yet back to work (currently on medical leave, appropriate). She is living at home with husband support, which is optimal for post-transplant monitoring and adherence.
 - Plan: Physical therapy and occupational therapy referral to support deconditioning recovery. Discuss return-to-work timeline at future visits (target approximately month 4-5 if recovery continues on current trajectory).

11. MIDOSTAURIN POST-TRANSPLANT:
Midostaurin (FLT3 inhibitor, PKC412) was used as part of induction and bridging consolidation per RATIFY trial standard of care. Post-transplant FLT3 inhibitor maintenance (e.g., gilteritinib or sora maintenance post-alloHSCT) is not currently part of our institutional protocol at this time given the pre-existing clinical data landscape; this will be reassessed as evidence matures. Patient is not currently on midostaurin or any targeted therapy post-transplant.
 - Plan: Continue to monitor emerging data on post-transplant FLT3 inhibitor maintenance strategies. Raise at tumor board if MRD status or chimerism raises any concern at day +60.

12. CARE COORDINATION AND FOLLOW-UP:
Patient has been given detailed written and verbal instructions on signs and symptoms requiring urgent evaluation (fever >38.0°C, new rash, jaundice, significant diarrhea, bleeding, dyspnea, visual changes). Emergency contact numbers provided. She understands to present to the emergency department or call the transplant clinic immediately for any concerning symptoms.

RETURN VISIT:
Day +45 post-transplant: CBC, CMP, tacrolimus trough, CMV PCR, magnesium, phosphorus, LFTs. Assessment for GVHD, CMV response, electrolyte status.
Day +60 post-transplant: Bone marrow biopsy (MRD by MFC and FLT3-ITD PCR, cytogenetics, DNMT3A R882H ddPCR, chimerism on sorted CD34+ progenitors). Peripheral blood chimerism. Full labs. Clinic assessment.

Documentation time: Approximately 45 minutes for visit and note completion.

Electronically signed,
[Attending Hematologist / BMT Physician]
Blood and Marrow Transplant Program
Hematologic Malignancies Service
